page: UpCloud DEV-1xCPU-2GB is an entry-level virtual server belonging to the Developer family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features a single shared vCPU mapped to one physical core and one thread, utilizing an AMD EPYC 7542 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 2.0 GB of x86_64 memory and includes 30 GB of local SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate that this server is suited primarily for lightweight tasks. The shared AMD EPYC processor delivers a stress-ng single-core and multi-core score of 739.58, placing it in the bottom 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with read-only large block speeds at 9.08 GB/sec and write speeds at 7.31 GB/sec. However, single-threaded compression tasks show moderate capability, with gzip compression reaching 24.3 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 8.78 GB/sec, both scoring in the middle 50%.

Due to its shared CPU allocation and low memory capacity, the server exhibits poor performance in database operations with a PassMark database score of 524.6 and static web serving throughput of 1.13 GB/sec. It represents a highly cost-efficient option for non-production environments, software development, testing, and lightweight utility scripts where continuous high-performance compute resources are not required.
description: UpCloud DEV-1xCPU-2GB is an entry-level virtual server in the Developer family, utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is configured with a single shared vCPU from an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, 2.0 GB of memory, and 30 GB of local SSD storage. Benchmark results show that the server sits in the bottom 10% for most compute and memory metrics, including a stress-ng score of 739.58 and a PassMark database operations score of 524.6. However, it achieves average tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression at 24.3 GB/sec. Given its shared resource model and low hardware footprint, this server is a cost-effective option designed for development, testing, and lightweight utility workloads rather than performance-critical production applications.
og_description: UpCloud DEV-1xCPU-2GB is an entry-level KVM virtual server featuring 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 30 GB of SSD storage, designed for lightweight development and testing workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud DEV-1xCPU-2GB is a KVM virtual server with 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU, 2.0 GB RAM, and 30 GB SSD storage, built for basic development tasks.
tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-resource virtual server with AMD EPYC processing and local SSD storage for basic development and testing.

2.0 GB of system memory and 30 GB of local SSD storage
KVM hypervisor-based virtualization on x86_64 architecture
Best suited for non-production development, testing, and basic scripts
